option: Cafe/restaurant: warm earth palette + editorial food photography + asymmetric menu grid
best_for: Local cafes bakeries and restaurants wanting an inviting non-chain feel that still reads premium
avoid_when: A fast-casual chain wanting a clean high-volume ordering flow where warmth would slow conversion
tradeoffs: Builds appetite appeal and locality fast; genuinely good food photography is a real production cost not a template swap

option: Healthcare/clinic: muted sage-and-clay or cool-clinical palette + generous whitespace + calm serif headings
best_for: Clinics wellness practices and healthcare providers needing to signal trust and calm without looking cold or corporate
avoid_when: Emergency or urgent-care contexts where speed-to-action beats calm aesthetics
tradeoffs: Reduces the sterile hospital-brochure look competitors default to; whitespace discipline requires real content editing not just CSS

option: Legal/law firm: deep ink palette + serif display type + structured grid signaling authority
best_for: Law firms and professional services wanting gravitas and credibility over playful branding
avoid_when: A boutique or consumer-facing legal service wanting approachability over authority
tradeoffs: Signals seriousness and competence fast; can tip into generic-corporate if the type pairing isn't deliberately chosen (see UX024)

option: SaaS/tech: bold accent-on-neutral + product screenshots as hero content + confident type scale
best_for: B2B and B2C SaaS wanting the product itself to be the primary visual proof not illustration
avoid_when: Pre-product-market-fit landing pages with nothing real to screenshot yet
tradeoffs: Product-as-hero builds credibility fastest for software; requires the actual product UI to already look good

option: E-commerce/retail: high-contrast product photography grid + urgency-aware color blocking
best_for: Retail and e-commerce wanting fast scannability and clear purchase-path visual hierarchy
avoid_when: Luxury retail where urgency tactics undercut the premium positioning (use UX007's jewel-tone approach instead)
tradeoffs: Optimizes for conversion-path clarity; can read generic-Shopify if the grid and color-blocking aren't customized past the template defaults

option: Real estate: large-format imagery + minimal chrome + map-integrated browsing
best_for: Real estate and property listings where the inventory itself is the visual content
avoid_when: A real-estate SaaS tool for agents where the product not listings is the hero (use UX071 instead)
tradeoffs: Lets genuinely good property photography carry the design; weak photography has nowhere to hide in this pattern

option: Fitness/wellness: high-energy duotone or gradient + dynamic diagonal layout + bold sans display type
best_for: Gyms fitness apps and wellness brands wanting motion and energy conveyed even in static layouts
avoid_when: A clinical wellness/medical-adjacent brand wanting calm over energy (use UX069 instead)
tradeoffs: Conveys momentum and motivation visually; diagonal/dynamic layouts need real design discipline to stay legible not chaotic

option: Finance/fintech: single-hue data-dense palette + tabular numeric precision + restrained motion
best_for: Fintech and financial services needing to convey precision and trust through information design itself
avoid_when: A consumer fintech onboarding flow wanting warmth and approachability over pure data precision
tradeoffs: Precision-as-aesthetic differentiates from both stuffy legacy-bank and overly playful neobank extremes; requires real data design discipline (tabular figures UX030 numeric alignment)

option: Education/e-learning: friendly rounded type + progress-forward layout + optimistic color use
best_for: Online courses schools and e-learning platforms wanting approachable, achievement-oriented design
avoid_when: Elite/prestige-positioned academic institutions wanting gravitas over approachability (lean toward UX070's authority pattern instead)
tradeoffs: Lowers the intimidation barrier to starting and completing courses; can read juvenile if rounded/optimistic choices aren't balanced with real typographic discipline

option: Nonprofit/cause-driven: authentic documentary photography + restrained single-accent palette + narrative-first layout
best_for: Nonprofits and cause-driven organizations needing to build trust and emotional connection without looking exploitative
avoid_when: Any context where the cause itself should not be visually dramatized for effect
tradeoffs: Authenticity over polish builds more durable trust in this category than production value; stock-photo shortcuts are especially visible and damaging here
